name: BFG
description: Senior Software Engineer building cross-platform desktop and mobile applications, with a focus on virtualization. Architect and ship shared codebases that target Windows, macOS and Linux from a single source — wiring up native modules where the platform demands it and keeping the UX consistent everywhere else. Own the virtualization layer end-to-end: container and VM lifecycle, guest/host bridges, and the performance work to keep the user-facing app responsive on top of it. Work directly with product and the rest of engineering to take features from spec through release.
tags: cross-platform, virtualization, electron, c++, native-modules, windows, macos, linux, ios, android

name: Digital Threads
description: Joined as Senior Full-Stack Developer and grew into the Team Lead role over a year and a half. Drove the technical roadmap, led architecture decisions and code review across the engineering org, and mentored mid-level engineers on shipping production features. Hands-on across the stack — Node.js and NestJS on the backend, Vue and React on the front end, PostgreSQL and MongoDB for storage, with REST API contracts and Redis caching tying the services together. Owned CI/CD pipelines on AWS and worked closely with product on scoping, estimation and delivery.
tags: team-lead, full-stack, node, nestjs, vue, react, typescript, postgresql, mongodb, redis, rest, aws, ci/cd

name: ArmBoldMind
description: Front-End Team Lead for a multi-product engineering organisation. Set the UI architecture standard across Angular and Vue codebases, ran technical interviews and onboarding for new front-end hires, and unblocked the team on cross-cutting concerns — state management, design-system adoption, accessibility, and Webpack/Vite tooling. Paired with backend engineers on API contracts, and reported on team delivery and quality metrics to leadership.
tags: team-lead, frontend, angular, vue, typescript, javascript, webpack, vite, design-system, accessibility, state-management

name: ECRO
description: Senior Full-Stack Developer responsible for end-to-end delivery of client platforms. Built REST APIs in Node.js and PHP backed by MySQL and PostgreSQL, integrated Elasticsearch for search and Redis for caching, and shipped the front-end in Angular and React. Handled deployment and infrastructure on AWS and Linux servers, and worked directly with stakeholders to translate product requirements into shippable features.
tags: full-stack, node, php, angular, react, mysql, postgresql, elasticsearch, redis, rest, aws, linux

name: EraSofts
description: Full-Stack Developer for nearly three years across the broadest range of stacks in the timeline. Delivered Laravel, Symfony, CodeIgniter and Yii backends for a rotating client roster, and the matching front-ends in AngularJS, Angular 2+, Vue and React. Built and integrated REST APIs, owned MySQL and MariaDB schemas, and operated CMS-driven sites on WordPress, Magento and OpenCart. The role demanded fast context-switching between client projects and shipping production-quality code on tight timelines.
tags: full-stack, php, laravel, symfony, codeigniter, yii, angularjs, angular, vue, react, mysql, mariadb, wordpress, magento, opencart, rest

name: SoftCode
description: First leadership role — Team Leader and PHP Expert for an agency delivering Laravel, Symfony, CodeIgniter and CakePHP applications. Scoped projects, broke work down for the team, ran code review, and stayed hands-on with the harder backend problems. Led several Magento and Drupal builds, designed MySQL schemas for B2B platforms, and stabilised legacy codebases inherited from earlier vendors. This is where the merge of "usability and user experience with technical knowledge" took shape as a working method.
tags: team-lead, php, laravel, symfony, codeigniter, cakephp, magento, drupal, mysql, b2b

name: Iguan Systems
description: First professional role after university. Shipped CMS-driven sites on WordPress, Joomla and Drupal, wrote custom PHP backends backed by MySQL, and built the matching front-ends in HTML5, CSS/SASS and jQuery. Picked up the discipline of writing maintainable code, working in Git, deploying to Linux servers, and reading other people's code without breaking it — the skills that carried into every role after this one.
tags: web-developer, php, mysql, wordpress, joomla, drupal, html5, css, sass, jquery, git, linux

name: Freelance
description: Independent engagements between and around full-time roles — through Upwork, Freelancer, PeoplePerHour and direct clients. Projects ranged from one-off WordPress and Shopify customisations to full-stack web apps in Node/React and React Native mobile builds. Owned scoping, estimation, delivery and client communication end-to-end, which sharpened the product instincts that carry across to my full-time work.
tags: freelance, full-stack, node, react, react-native, vue, typescript, wordpress, shopify, mobile

name: Open Source
description: GitHub portfolio of personal projects, experiments and contributions. Demonstrates working knowledge of the modern Node ecosystem — NestJS services, Vue 3 and React apps, React Native mobile builds, plus utilities and tooling around PostgreSQL, MongoDB and Redis. The repos are the most direct way to see how I actually write code outside a client brief.
tags: open-source, node, nestjs, vue, react, react-native, typescript, postgresql, mongodb, redis

name: Education
description: BA in IT Security from the National Polytechnic University of Armenia (2011-2015), preceded by a science-and-mathematics-focused high school. The security background informs how I think about authentication, authorisation and data handling in every project — and the maths foundation is what made the full-stack and graphics work I picked up later actually approachable.
tags: education, bachelor, it-security, cryptography, networking, algorithms, mathematics
